zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir James De Bellers於1335年出生在英國萊希斯特斯爾梅爾敦 莫布雷時，他的父親Ralph De Bellers II30歲，母親Agatha De Bingham25歲。他於1370年與Margaret Bernake在英國林可爾史爾塔特沙拉爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1356年被列為member of parliment。他去世於1411年在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾享年76歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir James De Bellers于1335年出生在英国萊希斯特斯爾梅爾敦 莫布雷时，他的父亲Ralph De Bellers II30岁，母亲Agatha De Bingham25岁。他于1370年与Margaret Bernake在英国林可爾史爾塔特沙拉爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他于1356年被列为member of parliment。他去世于1411年在英国英格兰萊希斯特斯爾享年76岁，埋葬在英国英格兰林可爾史爾。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bellers
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Bellairs
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of Bellars , suggesting French belle(s) aire(s) ‘lovely demeanor’.

variants.name: Bellars
meanings: (of Norman origin): apparently from Old French bélier ‘ram’, hence a nickname for someone thought to resemble a ram in some way or possibly a metonymic occupational name for a shepherd. In Northamptonshire the name attracts excrescent -s already by 1377.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir James De Bellers was born in 1335, in Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, England, his father, Ralph De Bellers II, was 30 and his mother, Agatha De Bingham, was 25. He married Margaret Bernake in 1370, in Tattershall, Lincolnshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. In 1356, at the age of 21, his occupation is listed as member of parliment. He died in 1411, in Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 76, and was buried in Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir James De Bellers est né en 1335, à Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, Angleterre, son père, Ralph De Bellers II, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Agatha De Bingham, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Bernake en 1370, à Tattershall, Lincolnshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. En 1356, à l’âge de 21 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est member of parliment. Il est décédé en 1411, à Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 76 ans, et a été enterré à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.Kirby Bellars, Melton Mowbray, Leicester: Kirby Bellars, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Sir James De Bellers 1335 in Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Ralph De Bellers II 30 und seine Mutter Agatha De Bingham 25. Er hat 1370 in Tattershall, Lincolnshire, England Margaret Bernake geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. 1356, als er 21 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als member of parliment aufgeführt. Er starb 1411 im Alter von 76 Jahren in Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

Lui sposato Margaret Bernake nel 1370, nel Tattershall, Lincolnshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Nel 1356, all'età di 21 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come member of parliment. Morì nel 1411, nel Leicestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 76 anni, e fu sepolto a Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir James De Bellersが1335年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアメルトン モウブレイで生まれた時，父Ralph De Bellers IIは30歳，母Agatha De Binghamは25歳であった。1370年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアタッテルシャルでMargaret Bernakeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。1356年，21歳の時，職業はmember of parlimentと記録されている。彼は1411年にイギリスイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアで76歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir James De Bellers(이)가 1335년 영국 레이스스터실르 멜턴 모우브라이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ralph De Bellers II(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Agatha De Bingham(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1370년 영국 링코실르 타터샤에서 Margaret Bernake(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1356년, 21세의 나이로 그의 직업은 member of parliment이었다. 그는 1411년 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르에서 76세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir James De Bellers nasceu em 1335, em Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Ralph De Bellers II, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Agatha De Bingham, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Bernake em 1370, em Tattershall, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Em 1356, com 21 anos, sua profissão aparece como member of parliment. Ele faleceu em 1411, em Leicestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 76 anos, e foi sepultado em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir James De Bellers родился в 1335 г. в местности мелтон мавбрэи, лисестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени Ralph De Bellers II было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Agatha De Bingham, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Bernake в 1370 г. в местности таттиршолл, линколншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. В 1356 году, в возрасте 21 год,  он работал по профессии member of parliment. Он умер в 1411 г. в местности лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 76 лет и был похоронен в местности линколншир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir James De Bellers nació en 1335, en Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Ralph De Bellers II, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Agatha De Bingham, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Margaret Bernake en 1370, en Tattershall, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. En 1356, a la edad de 21 años, su ocupación registrada era member of parliment. Murió en 1411, en Leicestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 76 años, y fue sepultado en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
